changes_made: Renamed 'k8s_pod_disaster_recovery_plan_documented' to 'k8s_cluster_disaster_recovery_plan_documented' for clarity and scope accuracy
review_notes: The functions generally map well to the control CP-10(6) focusing on system recovery and reconstitution. The naming convention is mostly followed, but a minor adjustment was made for clarity. The function 'k8s_pod_disaster_recovery_plan_documented' was renamed to 'k8s_cluster_disaster_recovery_plan_documented' to better reflect the broader scope of disaster recovery planning at the cluster level rather than individual pods.
naming_improvements: Ensure the scope of disaster recovery plans is accurately reflected in the function name to avoid confusion.
title: System Recovery And Reconstitution - System recovery and reconstitution | component protection protect system compone
step1_reasoning: The control CP-10(6) focuses on system recovery and reconstitution, emphasizing component protection. In Kubernetes, this translates to ensuring that critical components like etcd have backups configured, and that there are documented disaster recovery plans for pods and clusters. Automated snapshots for cluster state and configurations can help in reconstitution. Additionally, configuring pod anti-affinity can protect against node failures by ensuring pods are distributed across multiple nodes.
